title: Identify the Product (Work Management, Dev, CRM...) Where an App Is Installed/Used
excerpt: As app developers, we’ve noticed that apps can behave differently depending on which monday.com product (Work Management, Dev, CRM, etc.) they are used in. Right now, there’s no way for us to know where an app has been i…

posts.author: aleonmartinez (Angela Leon)
posts.cookedHtml:
As app developers, we’ve noticed that apps can behave differently depending on which monday.com product (Work Management, Dev, CRM, etc.) they are used in. Right now, there’s no way for us to know where an app has been installed, which makes it harder to:

  • Optimize the user experience – If we knew the product, we could fine-tune our app to work more smoothly in that environment.
  • Improve support & troubleshooting – Some issues might be product-specific, and having this information would help us address them more effectively.
  • Enhance marketing efforts – Understanding where our app is most popular could help us refine our messaging and outreach.

This information could be made available in the app context and also included in webhooks whenever an event is triggered.

This would be incredibly useful for us and other developers!
